title: Influence of Al content and precipitation state on the mechanical behavior of austenitic high-Mn low-density steels
abstract_summary: We investigate the strain hardening of two austenitic high-Mn low-density steels, Fe–30.5Mn–2.1Al–1.2C and Fe–30.5Mn–8.0Al–1.2C (wt.%), with different precipitation states. The strain hardening of the low-Al alloy is attributed to dislocation and twin substructures, while intergranular M3C-type carbides strongly affect fracture mode. The high-Al alloy's strain hardening and superior strength-ductility combination are linked to shearable nanosized κ-carbides that promote planar dislocation substructures. Detailed ECCI and TEM characterize deformation structures at different strain levels.
research_objective: To clarify the role of precipitation state in the strain hardening mechanisms of low-density Fe–Mn–Al–C steels.
main_process_variables: casting and hot-rolling: reheat 1200°C, hot-rolled 75% reduction at 1100°C, water quench
solution treatment: 1100°C for 2h under Ar, water quench
annealing treatments: short annealing 450°C/1h; long annealing 2Al: 650°C/72h, 8Al: 600°C/24h
tensile testing: room temperature, initial strain rate 5×10⁻⁴ s⁻¹
interrupted tensile tests to different strain levels for microstructure evolution
microscopy: ECCI and TEM on longitudinal sections (perpendicular to tensile axis)
expected_information_axis: mechanical properties (yield stress, ultimate tensile strength, uniform elongation, strain hardening stages)
deformation mechanisms (planar slip, twinning, shearing of κ-carbides)
key_sections: Results: Stress-strain behavior and mechanical properties
Results: Low Al content (2 wt.%) deformation structures
Results: High Al content (8 wt.%) deformation structures
